mission.title: kube-ovn: [Feature Request] Move vlan interfaces under ovs and create internal vlan interfaces
mission.description: [Feature Request] Move vlan interfaces under ovs and create internal vlan interfaces. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kube-ovn deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kube-ovn version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-ovn
helm list -n kube-system 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kube-ovn installation.

steps.title: Review kube-ovn configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kube-ovn configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-ovn
kubectl get configmap -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kube-ovn
```
Currently, when creating a ProviderNetwork that specifies a master interface (e.g., bond0), Kube-OVN moves the physical interface to an OVS bridge and transfers its IP configuration.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature Request] Move vlan interfaces under ovs and create…
steps.description:
# Pull Request

## What type of this PR
- Features
- Docs
- Tests

## Which issue(s) this PR fixes
```yaml
apiVersion: kubeovn.io/v1
kind: ProviderNetwork
metadata:
  name: net1
spec:
  defaultInterface: bond0
  # New field to enable VLAN sub-interface handling
  preserveVlanSubinterfaces: true
  # Optional: explicit list of VLAN IDs to preserve
  vlanSubinterfaces:
    - 10
    - 20
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-ovn
kubectl get events -n kube-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature Request] Move vlan interfaces under ovs and create…" is functioning correctly.

# Pull Request

## Which issue(s) this PR fixes
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: kubeovn.io/v1
kind: ProviderNetwork
metadata:
  name: net1
spec:
  defaultInterface: bond0
  # New field to enable VLAN sub-interface handling
  preserveVlanSubinterfaces: true
  # Optional: explicit list of VLAN IDs to preserve
  vlanSubinterfaces:
    - 10
    - 20

apiVersion: kubeovn.io/v1
kind: ProviderNetwork
metadata:
  name: eth0
spec:
  defaultInterface: eth0
  preserveVlanInterfaces: true

    link/ether 52:95:05:0e:0a:58 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.9.111.161/16 brd 10.9.255.255 scope global noprefixroute br-eth0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fd74:ca9b:3a09:868c:10:9:111:161/64 scope global noprefixroute
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::5095:5ff:fe0e:a58/64 scope link
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
42: br-eth0-vlan10: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 76:e0:e3:6b:49:6c brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff

    in

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with Kube-OVN installed as the CNI plugin.
